title: TC-VF60-DFU-001: Verify no unsolicited Charge Case firmware update popup appears on Home screen at app launch (up-to-date firmware)
steps: Precondition: Voyager Free 60+ is connected to the phone. Both earbuds and Charge Case have the latest firmware installed.
1. Fully close (force-kill) the Poly Studio Mobile app.
2. Reopen Poly Studio Mobile app.
3. Observe the Home screen (main device screen showing battery levels for Left Earbud, Right Earbud, and Charge Case).
4. Wait 10 seconds on the Home screen without tapping anything.
5. Check for any popup, toast, or banner message related to 'Charge Case firmware update' or 'firmware update available'.
expected: No popup, toast, banner, or modal dialog related to Charge Case firmware update appears on the Home screen at any point during the 10-second observation window. The Home screen displays only the standard device information and battery levels.

title: TC-VF60-DFU-002: Verify no unsolicited Charge Case firmware update popup appears on Home screen at app launch (outdated Charge Case firmware)
steps: Precondition: Voyager Free 60+ is connected to the phone. The Charge Case has outdated firmware (e.g., version 1.0.0 when 1.2.0 is available). Earbuds may be on any firmware version.
1. Fully close (force-kill) the Poly Studio Mobile app.
2. Reopen Poly Studio Mobile app.
3. Observe the Home screen (main device screen showing battery levels for Left Earbud, Right Earbud, and Charge Case).
4. Wait 15 seconds on the Home screen without tapping anything.
5. Check for any popup, toast, banner, or modal dialog related to 'Charge Case firmware update' or 'firmware update available'.
6. If a popup appears, note its content and dismiss it if possible.
7. Navigate to Device Settings → Software Update and verify the update status there.
expected: No unsolicited popup, toast, banner, or modal dialog related to Charge Case firmware update appears on the Home screen. The Charge Case firmware update availability is ONLY indicated within the Device Settings → Software Update screen, not via a transient Home screen popup.

title: TC-VF60-DFU-003: Verify Charge Case firmware update is exclusively accessible via Software Update tab, not via Home screen popup
steps: Precondition: Voyager Free 60+ is connected to the phone. Charge Case has outdated firmware (e.g., version 1.0.0 when 1.2.0 is available).
1. Open Poly Studio Mobile app and wait for Home screen to fully load.
2. Verify no popup appears on Home screen (if any appears, dismiss and report as bug).
3. Tap the menu icon (hamburger or gear icon) to access Device Settings.
4. Tap 'Software Update' from the Device Settings list.
5. Verify the Software Update screen displays the Charge Case firmware update availability (e.g., 'Charge Case firmware update available' or similar text).
6. Tap 'Update' or 'Update All' to initiate the Charge Case firmware update.
7. Verify the update progress is shown and completes successfully.
expected: The Charge Case firmware update is ONLY accessible via Device Settings → Software Update. No popup or prompt appears on the Home screen. The Software Update screen correctly shows the available Charge Case firmware update and allows the user to initiate it.

title: TC-VF60-DFU-004: Verify no transient 'Brief Charge Case firmware update' popup appears during app launch sequence
steps: Precondition: Voyager Free 60+ is connected to the phone. Charge Case has outdated firmware.
1. Fully close (force-kill) the Poly Studio Mobile app.
2. Reopen Poly Studio Mobile app and immediately observe the screen during the launch/loading sequence.
3. Pay close attention to any brief popup that may appear and disappear quickly (within 1-3 seconds) during the transition from splash screen to Home screen.
4. Record the screen (screen recording) for the first 10 seconds after app launch.
5. Review the recording frame-by-frame to detect any transient popup related to 'Charge Case firmware update' or 'Brief Charge Case firmware update'.
6. If a transient popup is detected, note its exact text, duration on screen, and whether it auto-dismisses.
expected: No transient popup, even one that appears and disappears quickly, related to 'Charge Case firmware update' or 'Brief Charge Case firmware update' is observed during the app launch sequence. The Home screen transitions cleanly from splash screen to the main device view without any intermediate popups.

title: TC-VF60-DFU-005: Verify Charge Case firmware update popup does not appear after app restart following completed update
steps: Precondition: Voyager Free 60+ is connected to the phone. Charge Case firmware update was previously completed successfully via Device Settings → Software Update.
1. Verify the Charge Case is on the latest firmware version (check in Device Settings → Software Update → Version Info).
2. Fully close (force-kill) the Poly Studio Mobile app.
3. Reopen Poly Studio Mobile app.
4. Observe the Home screen for 10 seconds.
5. Check for any popup, toast, or banner related to Charge Case firmware update.
6. Navigate to Device Settings → Software Update and confirm the status shows 'Up to date' for Charge Case.
expected: No popup related to Charge Case firmware update appears on the Home screen after app restart when the Charge Case firmware is already up to date. The Software Update screen confirms 'Up to date' status for the Charge Case.

title: TC-VF60-DFU-006: Verify Charge Case firmware update popup does not appear after Bluetooth disconnect/reconnect cycle
steps: Precondition: Voyager Free 60+ is connected to the phone. Charge Case has outdated firmware.
1. Open Poly Studio Mobile app and verify Home screen shows no popup.
2. Disconnect the Voyager Free 60+ via phone Bluetooth settings (tap 'Disconnect' or 'Forget').
3. Wait 5 seconds.
4. Reconnect the Voyager Free 60+ by placing earbuds in the Charge Case, closing the lid, then opening the lid.
5. Open Poly Studio Mobile app (if not auto-launched).
6. Observe the Home screen for 10 seconds for any popup related to Charge Case firmware update.
7. Navigate to Device Settings → Software Update and verify the update is listed there.
expected: No popup related to Charge Case firmware update appears on the Home screen after Bluetooth disconnect/reconnect. The update availability is only shown in Device Settings → Software Update.

title: TC-VF60-DFU-007: Verify Charge Case firmware update popup does not appear after phone reboot
steps: Precondition: Voyager Free 60+ is connected to the phone. Charge Case has outdated firmware.
1. Open Poly Studio Mobile app and verify Home screen shows no popup.
2. Close the Poly Studio Mobile app.
3. Reboot the phone.
4. After phone restarts, ensure Bluetooth is enabled.
5. Wait for Voyager Free 60+ to auto-reconnect (or manually reconnect via Bluetooth settings).
6. Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
7. Observe the Home screen for 10 seconds for any popup related to Charge Case firmware update.
8. Navigate to Device Settings → Software Update and verify the update is listed there.
expected: No popup related to Charge Case firmware update appears on the Home screen after phone reboot. The update availability is only shown in Device Settings → Software Update.

title: TC-VF60-DFU-008: Verify Charge Case firmware update popup does not appear when switching from another app back to Poly Studio Mobile
steps: Precondition: Voyager Free 60+ is connected to the phone. Charge Case has outdated firmware.
1. Open Poly Studio Mobile app and verify Home screen shows no popup.
2. Press the Home button to background the Poly Studio Mobile app.
3. Open another app (e.g., Settings, Chrome, or Phone) and use it for 10 seconds.
4. Switch back to Poly Studio Mobile app via the app switcher or recent apps.
5. Observe the Home screen for 10 seconds for any popup related to Charge Case firmware update.
6. Navigate to Device Settings → Software Update and verify the update is listed there.
expected: No popup related to Charge Case firmware update appears on the Home screen when returning to the app from background. The update availability is only shown in Device Settings → Software Update.
